name: cellphonedb
label: CellPhoneDB
summary: CellPhoneDBv2 calculates a mean of ligand-receptor expression as a measure of interaction magnitude, along with a permutation-based p-value as a measure of specificity. Here, we use the former to prioritize interactions, subsequent to filtering according to p-value less than 0.05.
description: CellPhoneDBv2 calculates a mean of ligand-receptor expression as a measure of interaction magnitude, along with a permutation-based p-value as a measure of specificity. Here, we use the former to prioritize interactions, subsequent to filtering according to p-value less than 0.05.

additional_info.paper_name: CellPhoneDB: inferring cell–cell communication from combined expression of multi-subunit ligand–receptor complexes

name: connectome
label: Connectome
summary: Connectome uses the product of ligand-receptor expression as a measure of magnitude, and the average of the z-transformed expression of ligand and receptor as a measure of specificity.
description: Connectome uses the product of ligand-receptor expression as a measure of magnitude, and the average of the z-transformed expression of ligand and receptor as a measure of specificity.

additional_info.paper_name: Computation and visualization of cell–cell signaling topologies in single-cell systems data using Connectome

name: log2fc
label: Log2FC
summary: logFC (implemented in LIANA and inspired by iTALK) combines both expression and magnitude, and represents the average of one-versus-the-rest log2-fold change of ligand and receptor expression per cell type.
description: logFC (implemented in LIANA and inspired by iTALK) combines both expression and magnitude, and represents the average of one-versus-the-rest log2-fold change of ligand and receptor expression per cell type.

additional_info.paper_name: Comparison of methods and resources for cell-cell communication inference from single-cell RNA-Seq data

name: magnitude_rank_aggregate
label: Magnitude Rank Aggregate
summary: RobustRankAggregate generates a consensus rank of all methods implemented in LIANA providing either specificity or magnitude scores.
description: RobustRankAggregate generates a consensus rank of all methods implemented in LIANA providing either specificity or magnitude scores.

additional_info.paper_name: Comparison of methods and resources for cell-cell communication inference from single-cell RNA-Seq data

name: natmi
label: NATMI
summary: NATMI uses the product of ligand-receptor expression as a measure of magnitude. As a measure of specificity, NATMI proposes $specificity.edge = \frac{l}{l_s} \cdot \frac{r}{r_s}$; where $l$ and $r$ represent the average expression of ligand and receptor per cell type, and $l_s$ and $r_s$ represent the sums of the average ligand and receptor expression across all cell types. We use its specificity measure, as recommended by the authors for single-context predictions.
description: NATMI uses the product of ligand-receptor expression as a measure of magnitude. As a measure of specificity, NATMI proposes $specificity.edge = \frac{l}{l_s} \cdot \frac{r}{r_s}$; where $l$ and $r$ represent the average expression of ligand and receptor per cell type, and $l_s$ and $r_s$ represent the sums of the average ligand and receptor expression across all cell types. We use its specificity measure, as recommended by the authors for single-context predictions.

additional_info.paper_name: Predicting cell-to-cell communication networks using NATMI

name: random_events
label: Random Events
summary: Random generation of cell-cell communication events by random selection of ligand, receptor, source, target, and score
description: Random generation of cell-cell communication events by random selection of ligand, receptor, source, target, and score

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis

name: singlecellsignalr
label: SingleCellSignalR
summary: SingleCellSignalR provides a magnitude score as $LRscore = \frac{\sqrt{lr}}{\mu+\sqrt{lr}}$; where $l$ and $r$ are the average ligand and receptor expression per cell type, and $\mu$ is the mean of the expression matrix.
description: SingleCellSignalR provides a magnitude score as $LRscore = \frac{\sqrt{lr}}{\mu+\sqrt{lr}}$; where $l$ and $r$ are the average ligand and receptor expression per cell type, and $\mu$ is the mean of the expression matrix.

additional_info.paper_name: SingleCellSignalR: inference of intercellular networks from single-cell transcriptomics

name: specificity_rank_aggregate
label: Specificity Rank Aggregate
summary: RobustRankAggregate generates a consensus rank of all methods implemented in LIANA providing either specificity or magnitude scores.
description: RobustRankAggregate generates a consensus rank of all methods implemented in LIANA providing either specificity or magnitude scores.

additional_info.paper_name: Comparison of methods and resources for cell-cell communication inference from single-cell RNA-Seq data

name: true_events
label: True Events
summary: Perfect prediction of cell-cell communication events from target data
description: Perfect prediction of cell-cell communication events from target data

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis
